story.premise: Three days before the inaugural lighting ceremony of the Eiffel Tower, the player — an assistant to Gustave Eiffel — discovers signs of deliberate sabotage to the tower's electrical system. A coalition of nationalist politicians and foreign industrial rivals intends to humiliate France by extinguishing the lights of the Exposition Universelle on its most visible night.

setting.venue: Exposition Universelle — Champ de Mars and the Eiffel Tower
period_details: Gas and arc lighting in transition to electric
Third Republic political instability, Boulangist movement at peak
Franco-Prussian War memory still raw — anti-German sentiment strong
Telegraph, pneumatic post, horse-drawn carriages, early telephony
International press covering the Exposition daily

story.real_world_inspiration: The 1889 Exposition Universelle and the opening of the Eiffel Tower, loosely adapted with fictional conspirators. The Boulangist political movement's anti-establishment nationalism provides authentic backdrop. The tower's actual electrical illumination debut is the fulcrum.

conspiracy.motivation: A coalition of French Boulangist nationalists — who consider the tower a vulgar industrial monument unworthy of France — and an Austrian engineering consortium that lost the Exposition contract to Eiffel. The nationalists want public humiliation of the Republican government; the Austrians want to demonstrate that Eiffel's design is unreliable.
conspiracy.sabotage_plan: Replace the primary electrical relay assembly on the second platform with a visually identical but internally sabotaged unit. Under full load during the lighting ceremony, the relay fails, triggering a cascade that blacks out the tower and risks an electrical fire visible to the entire Exposition crowd and international press.

timeline.T_minus_3_days: Wenzel obtains maintenance access via a forged work order
timeline.T_minus_2_days: Sabotaged relay is installed in the early morning hours
timeline.T_minus_1_day: Player's investigation begins after an engineer notices anomalies
timeline.opening_night: Ceremony begins at 21:00 — sabotage triggers at first full illumination load

conspiracy.fail_safe: If Wenzel is caught early, the intermediary carries a backup plan to bribe a maintenance worker to introduce a secondary fault the night of the ceremony.

npcs.name: Gustave Eiffel
npcs.role: Chief engineer and director of the tower project; the player's superior
npcs.public_face: Confident, visionary, under enormous pressure to deliver a flawless opening
npcs.hidden_agenda: Has quietly suppressed a prior electrical fault report to avoid bad press before the Exposition opens; fears the press more than the sabotage
knowledge: An unusual maintenance request was filed two days ago
A German-speaking engineer was cleared for access by a forged credential
The relay assembly was recently 'inspected' without his authorization
npcs.behavior_under_pressure: Deflects, rationalizes, and delays action unless presented with irrefutable physical evidence; responds to calm precision, not alarm

npcs.name: Franz Wenzel
npcs.role: Austrian electrical engineer attached to a competing pavilion exhibit
npcs.public_face: Collegial, technically impressive, mildly condescending about French engineering
npcs.hidden_agenda: Was hired by the Austrian consortium to execute the relay substitution; motivated by professional rivalry and a substantial payment
knowledge: Exactly which relay unit was swapped and where it is stored
The identity of the intermediary who hired him
The precise failure mode of the sabotaged relay
npcs.behavior_under_pressure: Deflects with technical jargon and counter-questions; becomes hostile if shown documentary evidence; will attempt to flee if cornered with physical proof

npcs.name: Colette Moreau
npcs.role: Journalist for Le Figaro with unofficial access to fair staff areas
npcs.hidden_agenda: Wants the story — will help the player if she believes the tip is real, but will publish if the player tries to bury it
knowledge: Heard a rumor of foreign money circulating among fair workers
Knows a hotel concierge who overheard a suspicious meeting
Has a list of after-hours access logs she obtained informally
npcs.behavior_under_pressure: Becomes an ally if treated honestly; becomes a threat if manipulated; her publication of a partial story can destabilize the investigation

npcs.name: Marcel Dupont
npcs.hidden_agenda: Has been accepting cash payments to allow after-hours access to a wine smuggling operation using the tower's freight lift — unrelated to the sabotage
knowledge: Someone used the maintenance freight access two nights ago
He did not witness who it was — he was paid to be elsewhere
He knows a relay inspection was logged without his sign-off
npcs.behavior_under_pressure: Panics and looks guilty because of the smuggling; will confess the smuggling if cornered, which clears him but wastes the player's time — a deliberate false lead

npcs.name: Sophie Lacroix
npcs.role: Socialite with press credentials and access to restricted levels through a romantic connection to a fair official
npcs.hidden_agenda: Was on the restricted second platform after hours for a private meeting with her lover — she saw Wenzel but assumed he was legitimate maintenance
knowledge: Saw a man matching Wenzel's description near the relay room after midnight
He carried a large leather case and spoke to no one
She has not reported it because she does not want her presence there known
npcs.behavior_under_pressure: Evasive about her own presence; will give testimony if the player can assure her discretion; can be the key eyewitness

npcs.name: Victor Mühlberg
npcs.role: Private agent acting for the Boulangist political network; no official Exposition role
npcs.public_face: Presents as a Swiss art dealer attending the Exposition
npcs.hidden_agenda: Coordinated financing and access for the sabotage; handled contact between the French political backers and Wenzel
knowledge: The names of the political backers
The complete sabotage plan including the backup fault option
Wenzel's current location at all times
npcs.behavior_under_pressure: Calm, denies everything, will attempt to disappear from Paris if he believes he has been identified; has a forged Swiss passport prepared

clues.description: A maintenance work order for 'relay assembly inspection' on the second platform, signed by an engineer name that matches no authorized staff list.
clues.notes: The forged signature is a near-match for a real engineer who was on leave — intentional cover

clues.description: A hotel receipt showing Franz Wenzel dined privately with an unnamed guest on the night before the forged work order was filed.
clues.notes: The guest signed as 'M. Meunier' — a name Mühlberg used as an alias

clues.description: A ledger entry showing Marcel Dupont received cash payments for three consecutive nights of 'access facilitation.'
clues.notes: Payments are for wine smuggling access, not the sabotage — a false lead that consumes investigation time

clues.description: A draft letter, unsigned, calling the tower 'a monument to Republican vanity that deserves to be humbled before the eyes of Europe.'
clues.notes: Proves political motivation and Mühlberg's network but not direct operational involvement

clues.description: A relay assembly in a locked storage room that appears standard externally but has been internally rewired to fail under full load.
clues.notes: The rewiring technique is consistent with Austrian electrical engineering practice of the period

clues.description: Eiffel Tower electrical schematics found in Wenzel's workshop with handwritten annotations in German identifying load thresholds and failure points.
clues.notes: The annotations pre-date his official start date at the Exposition — he studied the system before arriving

clues.description: A restricted-level access log showing Sophie Lacroix signed in twice after midnight in the past week.
clues.notes: Her access was for personal reasons, not sabotage — but she witnessed Wenzel and can provide eyewitness testimony

clues.description: A folded note in partial cipher found in Wenzel's coat pocket, referencing 'la lumière' and a date corresponding to opening night.
clues.notes: The cipher is simple and period-consistent; the date and phrase confirm the target and timing

clues.description: An internal report from ten days prior flagging anomalies in the relay assembly — marked 'hold for review' and never acted on.
clues.notes: Not evidence of conspiracy — Eiffel suppressed it for press reasons — but reveals institutional vulnerability and unlocks Eiffel's cooperation

clues.description: A hotel concierge identifies 'M. Meunier' from the receipt as a man who paid in gold coin and asked specifically about engineering staff dining schedules.
clues.notes: Combined with the hotel receipt, confirms Mühlberg's operational role and his method of contact with Wenzel

clues.description: Sophie Lacroix, once persuaded to speak, confirms she saw a man matching Wenzel's description carrying a large case near the relay room at approximately 01:30.
clues.notes: Requires the player to first discover the access log and then gain her trust through discretion — a two-step unlock

clue_relationships.reveals: Wenzel planned the sabotage before arriving and forged his own authorization to execute it
Confirms both the method and Wenzel's technical authorship — sufficient for Eiffel to order the relay replaced and Wenzel detained
Mühlberg (alias Meunier) is the operational coordinator between the political network and Wenzel
Full political sponsorship chain — from Boulangist faction through Mühlberg to Wenzel
Sophie Lacroix is an eyewitness who can place Wenzel at the scene — requires two-step trust unlock
False lead — pursuing Dupont reveals wine smuggling, wastes time, and redirects suspicion away from Wenzel
Unlocks Eiffel's full cooperation once the player confronts him with it — he stops deflecting and authorizes full access
Confirms the ceremony is the target, the timing, and the coordination between Wenzel and the financing network

acts.name: The Anomaly
beats: Eiffel briefs the player on the anomalous relay inspection report
Player discovers the forged work order in the maintenance office
First suspect emerges: Dupont's payment record surfaces as an apparent lead
The player learns of the relay assembly on the second platform
acts.goal: Establish that deliberate interference has occurred and surface at least one lead

acts.name: Pressure and Misdirection
beats: Pursuing Dupont reveals the smuggling — a red herring that costs time
Wenzel surfaces as a credible suspect via the hotel receipt or blueprint annotations
Colette Moreau becomes involved — asset or risk depending on player's candor
Sophie Lacroix is identified as a potential witness but is evasive
Eiffel attempts to delay action to protect press perception
Mühlberg attempts to leave Paris if he senses investigation closing in
acts.goal: Identify Wenzel as the saboteur and establish the political connection

acts.name: The Ceremony
beats: Crowd assembles; ceremony is one hour away
Player must choose how to intervene: confront Wenzel, replace the relay, alert security, or involve Eiffel
Mühlberg's escape attempt or backup plan activates if not addressed
Resolution before the lights are switched on
acts.goal: Stop the sabotage before the ceremony; expose or contain the conspiracy

conditions: Sabotaged relay is identified and replaced before the ceremony
Wenzel is detained by fair security with physical evidence presented
Eiffel is warned and authorizes intervention in time
success.outcome: The tower is illuminated without incident. Eiffel quietly thanks the player. The political network is not publicly exposed but is known to authorities.

partial_success.label: Sabotage stopped, conspiracy hidden
conditions: Relay is replaced and ceremony succeeds
Mühlberg escapes before he can be identified
partial_success.outcome: The fair opens safely but the conspiracy's backers face no consequence. Colette Moreau suspects a story was buried.

partial_success.label: Conspiracy exposed, ceremony disrupted
conditions: Player goes public with Colette Moreau before replacing the relay
Ceremony is delayed or cancelled as a precaution
partial_success.outcome: The sabotage is prevented but the scandal dominates opening coverage. Eiffel is furious. The Boulangist network is politically embarrassed.

failure.label: Ceremony fails
conditions: Player does not locate and replace the relay before opening
Relay fails on activation — tower goes dark before international press
failure.outcome: A cascade failure blacks out the tower. No fire, but the humiliation is complete. The Republican government faces a political crisis.

failure.label: Time expired
conditions: Player is still pursuing Dupont or other false leads when the ceremony begins
failure.outcome: No intervention reaches Eiffel in time. The sabotage succeeds.

conditions: Player formally accuses Marcel Dupont of the sabotage
Dupont is detained but Wenzel remains free
false_accusation.outcome: Dupont confesses to smuggling but not sabotage. The real sabotage proceeds unimpeded. Eiffel loses trust in the player and withholds further access.
